concerns.text: T028 implements a sensitivity sweep over relative thresholds (1%, 5%, 10% of mean SASA), but FR-006 in spec.md explicitly mandates absolute thresholds {0.01, 0.05, 0.1} Å². The task order implements a different artifact than the spec requires, creating a consumer-producer mismatch where the evaluation logic (T028) does not consume the required spec-defined threshold values.

T021 implements a 'Random Forest Baseline using 2D topological descriptors'. However, FR-004 and US2 in spec.md mandate a 'Geometry-Based Baseline using RDKit's 3D conformer generation'. The task order places a 2D-only baseline implementation before the evaluation (T022-T025), meaning the downstream evaluation tasks will consume an artifact (2D baseline) that does not satisfy the spec's requirement for a 3D geometry baseline. This breaks the data-flow dependency required to test the core hypothesis.

The spec's Edge Cases section mandates that if >10% of conformer generation fails, the 'pipeline halts with a critical error'. T014 (conformer generation) and T016 (error handling) only describe logging and continuing. The task order fails to include a task that implements the mandatory 'halt' logic, meaning the downstream data split (T015) and model training (T020+) will consume a dataset that may be invalid according to the spec's safety constraints.

T010 and T011 are marked [P] (parallel-safe) and placed before T012-T017. While contract tests can theoretically run before implementation if schemas are defined, the 'Independent Test' for US1 requires the *output* of the pipeline (CSV/Parquet with specific columns). T011 (Integration test) cannot run successfully until T012-T014 are implemented. Marking T011 as [P] and placing it before implementation tasks implies a false parallel dependency; it should be ordered after the implementation tasks it validates or removed from the parallel block.
